title: SAX Callback Operations
text: SAX Callback Operations SAX callback operations are APIs that can be implemented on a user-defined SAX event handler. They enable you to use the parser information to manipulate and analyze XML data, and handle any errors that may be encountered. SAX stands for Simple API for XML. It is a standard for accessing information in XML documents using a programming language and a parser for that language. Originally for Java only, there are now versions for several programming language environments. XML namespaces are supported (on Windows, Unix and Linux), either by using the STARTPREFIXMAPPING or ENDPREFIXMAPPING operations in your SAX event handler, or by implementing the STARTELEMENT or ENDELEMENT operations with namespace-specific parameters. Operations For descriptions of the available operations, see the related topics. The SAX parser included with Uniface supports the following callback operations: Table 1. Supported SAX Callback Operations Operation Description STARTDOCUMENT Activated when the parser starts reading the document. ENDDOCUMENT Activated when the end of the document is reached. STARTPREFIXMAPPING Activated when the parser encounters a namespace ( xmlns ) specification in a start tag. ENDPREFIXMAPPING Activated for each namespace specification when the parser encounters the end tag for a start tag that holds a namespace specification. STARTELEMENT Activated when the parser encounters a start tag, for example <tagA> . ENDELEMENT Activated when the parser encounters an end tag such as </tagA> . CHARACTERS Activated when character data is encountered between a tag and its end tag. COMMENT Activated when an XML comment is encountered ( <!-- --> ). WHITESPACE Activated when the parser encounters character data that it considers to be ignorable whitespace. NOTATIONDECL Activated when the parser encounters a notation declaration in a DTD. RESOLVEENTITY Activated when the parser encounters external DTD declarations and unrecognized XML entity references. UNPARSEDENTITY Activated when the parser encounters entity declarations in a DTD that contain the NDATA keyword. PROCESSINGINST Activated when the parser encounters a processing instruction. $error Activated when the parser issues a recoverable error. FATALERROR Activated when the parser issues a non-recoverable error. WARNING Activated when the parser issues a warning. Related Links Creating a SAX Event Handler Before starting to parse XML documents using the UXMLREADER component, you need to implement the operations that handle parser events, such as starting and ending the document and elements, and error handling.
